title: Break Thou the Bread of Life
firstLine: Break Thou the Bread of Life,
publicDomainBasis: Mary Artemesia Lathbury (1841–1913), William Fiske Sherwin (1826–1888). All public domain.

sections.label: Verse 1
sections.text:
Break Thou the Bread of Life,
Dear Lord, to me,
As Thou didst break the loaves
Beside the sea;
Beyond the sacred page
I seek Thee, Lord;
My spirit pants for Thee,
O Living Word.

sections.label: Verse 2
sections.text:
Thou art the Bread of Life,
O Lord, to me,
Thy holy Word the truth
That saveth me;
Give me to eat and live
With Thee above;
Teach me to love Thy truth,
For Thou art Love.

sections.label: Verse 3
sections.text:
Oh, send Thy Spirit, Lord,
Now unto me,
That He may touch my eyes,
And make me see;
Show me the truth concealed
Within Thy Word,
And in Thy Book revealed
I see the Lord.

sections.label: Verse 4
sections.text:
Bless Thou the truth, dear Lord,
To me, to me,
As Thou didst bless the bread
By Galilee;
Then shall all bondage cease,
All fetters fall,
And I shall find my peace,
My All in all.
